all.en: Min-hsuan had been the quietest student in class for almost three full years.
all.zh: 敏萱已經當了將近三年班上最安靜的學生。

all.en: She ate lunch alone, walked home alone, and rarely spoke unless a teacher asked first.
all.zh: 她獨自吃午餐、獨自走路回家，除非老師先問，否則很少開口說話。

all.en: Her favorite place in the entire school was the library's quiet third-floor corner.
all.zh: 整個學校她最喜歡的地方是圖書館三樓那個安靜的角落。

all.en: There she could read for hours without anyone noticing or asking awkward questions.
all.zh: 在那裡她可以讀好幾個小時的書，沒有人會注意她或問尷尬的問題。

all.en: One Wednesday afternoon, a new librarian named Ms. Kao quietly took over the front desk.
all.zh: 一個星期三下午，一位名叫高老師的新圖書館員悄悄接手了前台。

all.en: Ms. Kao had kind eyes, gray streaks in her hair, and a soft, patient voice.
all.zh: 高老師有著親切的眼神、髮間夾雜的灰白髮絲，以及柔和而有耐心的嗓音。

all.en: She noticed Min-hsuan come in every single afternoon and head straight to the same chair.
all.zh: 她注意到敏萱每天下午都會進來，直接走向同一張椅子。

all.en: After two weeks, Ms. Kao left a small bookmark on Min-hsuan's usual table by surprise.
all.zh: 兩週後，高老師悄悄在敏萱常坐的桌上留了一張小書籤。

all.en: It said simply, I saved a new novel for you behind the desk, in neat handwriting.
all.zh: 上面工整地寫著，我在櫃台後幫你留了一本新小說。

all.en: Min-hsuan blushed deeply but walked over and quietly accepted the gift without speaking.
all.zh: 敏萱深深地臉紅，但走過去默默地接下了禮物，一句話也沒說。

all.en: The book was about a shy girl who learned to find her voice through writing poetry.
all.zh: 那本書講述一個害羞的女孩透過寫詩找到自己聲音的故事。

all.en: Min-hsuan finished the entire novel in three nights, hidden under her blanket with a flashlight.
all.zh: 敏萱在三個晚上裡讀完整本小說，躲在棉被下用手電筒照著看。

all.en: When she returned it, Ms. Kao asked her one simple question about the main character.
all.zh: 當她還書時，高老師問了她一個關於主角的簡單問題。

all.en: Surprisingly, Min-hsuan whispered an answer that was thoughtful, detailed, and almost poetic itself.
all.zh: 出乎意料地，敏萱輕聲回答，她的答案深思熟慮、詳細，本身幾乎就是一首詩。

all.en: Their short conversations slowly became weekly, then daily, then long and full of laughter.
all.zh: 她們的短暫對話慢慢變成每週一次，再變成每天，最後變得很長且充滿笑聲。

all.en: Ms. Kao recommended one careful book at a time, choosing each one with great attention.
all.zh: 高老師一次只推薦一本書，每一本都精心挑選。

all.en: She introduced Min-hsuan to poetry, mystery novels, science writing, and travel memoirs.
all.zh: 她讓敏萱認識了詩、推理小說、科學寫作和旅行回憶錄。

all.en: After one month, she invited Min-hsuan to help organize the library's small poetry corner.
all.zh: 一個月後，她邀請敏萱幫忙整理圖書館的小型詩歌角落。

all.en: Min-hsuan happily arrived early every day, dusting shelves and arranging books by mood.
all.zh: 敏萱每天都開心地早早到，撣去書架灰塵並依心情分類擺書。

all.en: Other students began noticing the new colorful display and stopping to browse curiously.
all.zh: 其他學生開始注意到新的繽紛展示，停下來好奇地瀏覽。

all.en: Ms. Kao gently suggested that Min-hsuan write her own short poem for the bulletin board.
all.zh: 高老師溫和地建議敏萱為公布欄寫一首自己的短詩。

all.en: The idea terrified her, but she stayed up all night writing eleven different versions.
all.zh: 這個想法讓她害怕，但她熬了一整夜寫了十一個不同版本。

all.en: Her final poem was about an empty chair that learned to feel sunlight again.
all.zh: 她最終的那首詩寫的是一張空椅子重新學會感受陽光。

all.en: Ms. Kao read it slowly, looked up, and told her it deserved a much bigger audience.
all.zh: 高老師慢慢讀完，抬起頭告訴她，這首詩值得讓更多人讀到。

all.en: She secretly entered the poem in the city's annual high school writing competition.
all.zh: 她偷偷把這首詩投稿到市政府年度高中寫作比賽。

all.en: Three weeks later, an official envelope arrived addressed personally to Min-hsuan at school.
all.zh: 三週後，一個官方信封寄到學校，收件人正是敏萱。

all.en: She had won third place, and her name would appear in the city's annual writing book.
all.zh: 她贏得了第三名，她的名字將出現在市政府年度寫作集裡。

all.en: The principal announced her name proudly in front of the entire morning assembly.
all.zh: 校長在全校晨會前驕傲地宣布了她的名字。

all.en: Min-hsuan walked to the stage with shaking knees but a small, real smile on her face.
all.zh: 敏萱雙膝顫抖地走上台，臉上掛著小小但真誠的微笑。

all.en: Classmates who had never spoken to her now wanted to read her award-winning poem.
all.zh: 從未跟她說過話的同學現在都想讀她得獎的詩。

all.en: Two girls from her literature class invited her to start a quiet writing group together.
all.zh: 她文學課的兩位女同學邀請她一起組一個安靜的寫作小組。

all.en: Min-hsuan agreed, surprising herself, and they met every Friday in that same library corner.
all.zh: 敏萱答應了，連她自己都很意外，她們每週五都在圖書館同一個角落聚會。

all.en: On graduation day, she gave Ms. Kao a small handwritten book of her thirty best poems.
all.zh: 畢業那天，她送給高老師一本手寫的小書，收錄了她最好的三十首詩。

all.en: Ms. Kao hugged her gently and told her that quiet voices often carry the loudest truths.
all.zh: 高老師輕輕擁抱她，告訴她安靜的聲音常常承載著最響亮的真理。

all.en: Years later, Min-hsuan returned as a published author and signed books in that same corner.
all.zh: 多年後，敏萱以出版作家的身分回到那個相同的角落為書迷簽書。

all.en: Ms. Kao, now retired, sat in the front row wearing the same patient smile from before.
all.zh: 已經退休的高老師坐在第一排，臉上掛著從前那同樣溫柔耐心的微笑。

all.en: The library had grown, but the same third-floor chair still waited quietly by the window.
all.zh: 圖書館變大了，但三樓那張椅子仍在窗邊靜靜地等候。

all.en: Min-hsuan dedicated her newest collection of poems to the librarian who first saw her.
all.zh: 敏萱將她最新的詩集獻給那位最早看見她的圖書館員。

all.en: A nervous young girl in the audience clutched a notebook and stared at the floor.
all.zh: 觀眾席中一位緊張的小女孩緊握著筆記本，盯著地板。

all.en: Min-hsuan recognized the look immediately and walked over to sit gently beside her.
all.zh: 敏萱立刻認出那種神情，走過去輕輕地坐在她身旁。

all.en: She asked the girl about her favorite book and listened with full, unhurried attention.
all.zh: 她問女孩最喜歡哪本書，並全神貫注、從容地傾聽。

all.en: The girl whispered her answer, and Min-hsuan signed her notebook with a small encouraging note.
all.zh: 女孩輕聲回答，敏萱在她的筆記本上簽名並寫下一句小小的鼓勵話語。

all.en: Outside the library, autumn leaves drifted gently down across the campus walkways.
all.zh: 圖書館外，秋葉沿著校園步道輕輕飄落。

all.en: Min-hsuan realized that her whole life had quietly begun with a single thoughtful bookmark.
all.zh: 敏萱領悟到，她的整個人生都因一張用心的小書籤而悄悄展開。

all.en: She walked home that evening determined to leave more bookmarks for strangers along the way.
all.zh: 那晚走回家的路上，她下定決心要為沿途的陌生人留下更多書籤。

all.en: Over the next year, Min-hsuan launched a small program called Quiet Voices in twelve schools.
all.zh: 在接下來的一年裡，敏萱在十二所學校推出了一個名為「安靜聲音」的小型計畫。

all.en: The program paired shy students with kind teachers who shared books and weekly handwritten notes.
all.zh: 該計畫將害羞的學生與親切的老師配對，他們會分享書籍和每週的手寫便條。

all.en: Ms. Kao came out of retirement briefly to help train the first group of teacher volunteers.
all.zh: 高老師短暫地從退休生活中復出，協助訓練第一批教師志工。

all.en: Letters from participating students filled three thick boxes within the program's very first semester.
all.zh: 在計畫第一個學期內，參與學生寄來的信就裝滿了三個厚厚的箱子。

all.en: One boy wrote that the program had given him the courage to speak in class for the first time.
all.zh: 一個男孩寫道，這個計畫給了他鼓起勇氣在課堂上第一次開口的力量。

all.en: Min-hsuan compiled the most touching letters into a small free pamphlet for libraries everywhere.
all.zh: 敏萱將最感人的信件編成一本免費的小冊子，發送到各地圖書館。

all.en: A national foundation noticed her efforts and offered a grant to expand the program nationwide.
all.zh: 一個全國性基金會注意到她的努力，提供了一筆補助金讓計畫擴展到全國。

all.en: She used the funding to print thousands of carefully chosen books for shy readers across the island.
all.zh: 她運用這筆經費為全島害羞的讀者印製了數千本精挑細選的書。

all.en: Each book contained a handwritten bookmark inviting the reader to write back if they ever wanted to.
all.zh: 每本書裡都夾著一張手寫書籤，邀請讀者只要願意就可以回信。

all.en: Min-hsuan answered every single reply personally, no matter how many letters arrived each week.
all.zh: 敏萱親自回覆每一封來信，不論每週收到多少封信都一樣。

all.en: She rented a tiny office above an old bookshop and turned it into the program's quiet headquarters.
all.zh: 她在一家舊書店樓上租了一間小辦公室，作為這個計畫安靜的總部。

all.en: Each volunteer received a small kit containing bookmarks, blank letter paper, and a careful handbook.
all.zh: 每位志工都會收到一個小工具包，裡面有書籤、空白信紙和一本細心的手冊。

all.en: Ms. Kao quietly mailed Min-hsuan a hand-knitted bookmark for every birthday for over a decade.
all.zh: 高老師十多年來，每年敏萱生日都會默默寄來一張手織書籤。

all.en: Min-hsuan saved each one in a wooden box she kept beside her writing desk for inspiration.
all.zh: 敏萱把每一張都收在書桌旁的木盒裡，作為靈感的來源。

all.en: On Ms. Kao's eightieth birthday, Min-hsuan threw her a surprise party in the school library corner.
all.zh: 在高老師八十歲生日當天，敏萱在學校圖書館的角落為她辦了一場驚喜派對。

all.en: Former Quiet Voices students flew in from five different countries to be there in person.
all.zh: 前「安靜聲音」計畫的學生們從五個不同的國家飛回來親自出席。

all.en: Ms. Kao read aloud one of Min-hsuan's earliest poems and made the entire room cry softly together.
all.zh: 高老師朗讀了敏萱最早的一首詩，讓整個房間的人一起輕聲落淚。

all.en: Min-hsuan published her seventh poetry collection that same autumn, with a quiet dedication on the first page.
all.zh: 同一個秋天，敏萱出版了她第七本詩集，第一頁有一句低調的獻詞。

all.en: The dedication read simply, for every librarian who pauses long enough to truly see a quiet child.
all.zh: 那句獻詞簡單地寫著：獻給每一位願意停下腳步真正看見安靜孩子的圖書館員。

all.en: She placed a copy on the third-floor library chair where her own story had quietly begun decades earlier.
all.zh: 她在三樓圖書館的那張椅子上放了一本書，數十年前她自己的故事就是從那裡悄悄展開的。

all.en: Ms. Kao noticed how Min-hsuan always borrowed thick books of poetry.
all.zh: 高老師注意到敏萱總是借閱厚厚的詩集。

all.en: One rainy afternoon, the librarian gently asked what she liked to read.
all.zh: 在一個下雨的午後，圖書館員溫柔地問她喜歡讀些什麼。

all.en: Min-hsuan was surprised that an adult truly wanted to hear her answer.
all.zh: 敏萱很驚訝，竟有大人真心想聽她的回答。

all.en: She began leaving short poems tucked between the returned library books.
all.zh: 她開始把短詩夾在歸還的圖書館書本之間。

all.en: Ms. Kao read each one carefully and wrote kind notes in the margins.
all.zh: 高老師仔細讀過每一首，並在頁邊寫下溫暖的話語。

all.en: For the first time, Min-hsuan felt that someone could truly see her.
all.zh: 敏萱第一次感覺到，有人能真正看見她。

all.en: The quiet corner slowly became a place where her thoughts felt safe.
all.zh: 那個安靜的角落漸漸成了她思緒能感到安全的地方。

all.en: She started speaking up in class, just a little more each week.
all.zh: 她開始在課堂上發言，每週都比前一週多一點點。

all.en: Ms. Kao encouraged her to enter a citywide poetry contest that spring.
all.zh: 高老師鼓勵她參加那年春天的全市詩歌比賽。

all.en: Years later, Min-hsuan returned to thank the woman who first believed in her.
all.zh: 多年後，敏萱回來感謝那位最初相信她的人。
